It’s traditionally served with bread for dipping. chickpeas onion garlic tomato harissa cumin olive oil lemon juice parsley bread salt spicy savoury tangy North African autumn winter spring

title: Algerian Stuffed Bell Peppers with Lamb and Rice
description: Colorful bell peppers stuffed with a savory mixture of ground lamb, rice, and aromatic spices. A satisfying dish with Mediterranean flavors.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Cut the tops off the bell peppers and remove seeds.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté chopped onion and garlic until soft.
Add ground lamb and cook until browned. Stir in paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Add rice, chopped tomatoes, and 300ml of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes until rice is partially cooked.
Stuff the bell peppers with the lamb and rice mixture. Place in a baking dish and pour 100ml of water into the dish.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 10 minutes until peppers are tender.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied red wine like a Merlot or a dry rosé.
tips: For a lighter version, substitute lamb with ground turkey.
Soak the bell peppers in cold water for 10 minutes to make them easier to handle when cutting.
title_en: Algerian Stuffed Bell Peppers with Lamb and Rice
embedding_text: Algerian Stuffed Bell Peppers with Lamb and Rice Colorful bell peppers stuffed with a savory mixture of ground lamb, rice, and aromatic spices. A satisfying dish with Mediterranean flavors. bell pepper ground lamb rice onion tomato garlic paprika cumin coriander parsley olive oil salt pepper savoury spicy herby North African spring summer autumn

title: Libyan Bazin with Eggs
description: A traditional Libyan dish featuring a rich barley dough topped with poached eggs and a spiced tomato sauce. Hearty and comforting, it’s a staple for breakfast or brunch.

steps.instruction: In a pot, bring 400ml of water to a boil. Gradually whisk in the barley flour until it forms a thick, smooth dough.
Reduce heat to low and continue stirring until the dough thickens further, about 5 minutes.
Shape the dough into a ball and place it in the center of a serving plate, flattening the top slightly.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté chopped onion, garlic, and chili pepper until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes, cumin, salt, and 100ml of water. Simmer for 15 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Make four small wells in the barley dough and crack an egg into each well. Spoon the tomato sauce over the top.
Cover the pan and cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes until the eggs are cooked to your liking. Garnish with parsley and serve hot.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a strong black coffee or a mint tea to complement the rich flavors.
tips: For extra richness, drizzle a little olive oil over the dish before serving.
If barley flour is unavailable, you can use whole wheat flour, though the texture will differ.
embedding_text: Libyan Bazin with Eggs A traditional Libyan dish featuring a rich barley dough topped with poached eggs and a spiced tomato sauce. Hearty and comforting, it’s a staple for breakfast or brunch. barley flour water egg onion tomato garlic chili pepper olive oil cumin salt parsley savoury spicy egg North African autumn winter spring

title: Egyptian Fattah with Rice and Eggplant
description: A unique and refreshing mix of rice, fried eggplant, crispy pita, and a garlicky yogurt sauce. This dish is a flavorful blend of textures and tastes.

steps.instruction: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside.
Cut eggplant into thin slices, salt them lightly, and let sit for 10 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry with a paper towel.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry eggplant slices until golden and crispy on both sides. Drain on paper towels.
Cut pita bread into small pieces and fry in the same oil until crispy. Drain on paper towels.
In a bowl, mix yogurt, minced garlic, lemon juice, vinegar, salt, and a little water to reach a pourable consistency.
In a large serving dish, layer the rice, crispy eggplant, fried pita, diced cucumber, and tomato.
Pour the yogurt sauce over the top and garnish with parsley. Serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a light white wine like a Viognier or a sparkling wine.
tips: For extra crunch, top with toasted almonds or pine nuts.
Use day-old pita bread for frying to ensure it crisps up nicely.
title_en: Egyptian Fattah with Rice and Eggplant
embedding_text: Egyptian Fattah with Rice and Eggplant A unique and refreshing mix of rice, fried eggplant, crispy pita, and a garlicky yogurt sauce. This dish is a flavorful blend of textures and tastes. rice aubergine pita bread yogurt garlic lemon juice vinegar cucumber tomato olive oil salt parsley creamy tangy refreshing North African spring summer

title: Moroccan Vegetable Tagine with Couscous
description: A fragrant slow-cooked vegetable stew with tender aubergines, courgettes, and chickpeas. Served with fluffy couscous for a hearty vegetarian meal.

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a tagine or deep pan. Sauté finely chopped red onion and minced garlic for 3 minutes until soft.
Add chopped aubergines and courgettes, stir well to coat in spices. Cook for 5 minutes until lightly browned.
Pour in tinned tomatoes, vegetable stock, chickpeas, and spices (cumin, coriander, paprika). Simmer on low heat for 20 minutes.
While tagine simmers, prepare couscous by pouring boiling water over it, cover, and let sit for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
Serve tagine hot over couscous, garnished with chopped fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a lightly chilled Algerian white wine like Muscat de Setif or a fruity Grenache-based rosé.
tips: Use a mix of root vegetables like carrots and parsnips for added heartiness.
If tagine is too thick, add a splash of water to maintain consistency.
embedding_text: Moroccan Vegetable Tagine with Couscous A fragrant slow-cooked vegetable stew with tender aubergines, courgettes, and chickpeas. Served with fluffy couscous for a hearty vegetarian meal. aubergine courgette red onion garlic cloves tinned tomatoes chickpeas raisins olive oil ground cumin ground coriander paprika vegetable stock couscous fresh coriander lemon savoury sweet spiced North African Moroccan spring autumn winter

title: Tunisian Lablabi with Chickpea Soup
description: A comforting chickpea soup infused with garlic, cumin, and harissa, traditionally served with crispy fried bread. A perfect spicy kick for cooler evenings.

steps.instruction: Cut bread into thick slices, drizzle with olive oil, and toast in the oven at 180°C for 10 minutes until crispy. Set aside.
In a pot, heat olive oil and sauté minced garlic for 1 minute. Add cumin and harissa, stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add chickpeas and vegetable stock. Simmer for 15 minutes until flavors meld. Season with salt and pepper.
Blend half the soup for a thicker texture (optional). Return to pot and heat through.
Serve hot in bowls, top with a drizzle of olive oil, lemon juice, chopped coriander, capers, and a hard-boiled egg. Serve with crispy bread on the side.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a bold Tunisian red like Coteaux d'Utique or a dry Algerian red such as Domaine de l'Ormes.
tips: Adjust harissa quantity to taste—start with 1 tbsp for mild heat.
For extra richness, add a spoonful of tahini to the soup.
embedding_text: Tunisian Lablabi with Chickpea Soup A comforting chickpea soup infused with garlic, cumin, and harissa, traditionally served with crispy fried bread. A perfect spicy kick for cooler evenings. chickpeas white bread garlic cloves ground cumin harissa paste olive oil lemon coriander leaves hard-boiled eggs capers vegetable stock spicy savoury citrusy North African Tunisian autumn winter

title: Arepa de Queso
description: A classic Venezuelan dish made with cornmeal and filled with cheese. Perfect for breakfast or a quick snack.

steps.instruction: Mix cornmeal, water, and salt to form a dough.
Divide dough into small balls and flatten into discs.
Heat oil in a pan and cook arepas until golden brown on both sides.
Insert cheese into the cooked arepas and serve warm.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with a light white wine like Sauvignon Blanc.
tips: Use pre-cooked cornmeal for convenience.
Press arepas gently to prevent cracking.
embedding_text: Arepa de Queso A classic Venezuelan dish made with cornmeal and filled with cheese. Perfect for breakfast or a quick snack. cornmeal water salt queso blanco oil savoury Latin American year-round

title: Pasta a la Huancaína
description: A creamy and spicy Peruvian pasta dish made with a cheese and chili sauce. A comforting meal for any occasion.

steps.instruction: Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Blend aubergine, yellow chili peppers, queso fresco, milk, salt, and oil to make the sauce.
Heat the sauce in a pan and mix with cooked pasta.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with an off-dry white wine like Gewürztraminer.
tips: Roast the yellow chili peppers to remove skins before blending.
Adjust chili quantity to taste for desired spice level.
embedding_text: Pasta a la Huancaína A creamy and spicy Peruvian pasta dish made with a cheese and chili sauce. A comforting meal for any occasion. pasta aubergine queso fresco yellow chili peppers milk salt oil coriander creamy spicy Latin American year-round

title: Feijoada Brasileira
description: A hearty Brazilian stew made with black beans and pork. A traditional dish often enjoyed on weekends.

steps.instruction: Soak black beans overnight. Drain and rinse.
Sauté bacon, onion, and garlic in oil until fragrant.
Add pork shoulder and brown on all sides.
Add beans, water, laurel leaves, and salt. Simmer for 1.5 hours.
Blend tomatoes with a bit of broth and add to stew. Cook for another 20 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with a full-bodied red wine like Malbec.
tips: Use a pressure cooker to reduce cooking time.
Add a splash of vinegar at the end to brighten flavors.

title: Ensalada de Nopales
description: A fresh and tangy Mexican salad made with cactus paddles, tomatoes, and coriander. A great light meal for warm days.

steps.instruction: Dice nopales and boil for 5 minutes to remove sliminess. Drain and rinse.
Dice tomato, onion, and cilantro. Mix in a bowl with nopales.
Whisk lime juice, olive oil, salt, and chili powder. Pour over salad and toss.
Chill for 15 minutes before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with a crisp white wine like Albariño.

Serve chilled for best texture.

title: Locro de Papa
description: A creamy Ecuadorian potato soup enriched with cheese and avocado. A comforting dish perfect for cooler evenings.

steps.instruction: Sauté onion and garlic in oil until translucent.
Add diced potatoes and courgette, cover with water, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Blend half the soup with milk and return to pot. Stir in queso fresco.
Blend avocado with a bit of broth and stir in. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with a medium-bodied white wine like Viognier.
tips: Use starchy potatoes for a creamier texture.
Add more milk for a lighter soup.

title: Arepa de Queso
description: A classic Venezuelan breakfast or snack made from ground maize dough. Best served warm with cheese inside for a rich, savoury flavour.

steps.instruction: Mix maize flour, water, and salt to form a dough. Knead until smooth.
Shape dough into small balls and flatten into discs. Stuff each with cheese.
Heat oil in a pan and cook arepas over medium heat until golden and cheese melts, about 3-4 minutes per side.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a light Colombian lager or a crisp white wine like Sauvignon Blanc.
tips: Use warm water for the dough for better consistency.
Press the arepa gently while cooking to ensure even melting of cheese.

title: Pabellón Criollo
description: Venezuela’s national dish featuring shredded beef, black beans, white rice, and fried sweet plantains. A hearty, balanced meal.

steps.instruction: Season beef with salt and cumin. Sear in oil until browned, then add chopped onion, garlic, and diced tomato. Simmer until tender (1.5 hours).
Cook rice separately with water and salt until fluffy.
Cook black beans with onion, garlic, and salt until soft. Mash slightly for texture.
Slice plantains lengthwise and fry in oil until caramelized.
Shred the beef and mix with some of its broth. Serve over rice, topped with beans, shredded beef, and plantains. Garnish with coriander.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a robust red wine like a Malbec or a Venezuelan red blend.
tips: Slow-cook the beef for maximum tenderness.
For extra flavour, add a splash of Worcestershire sauce to the beef while cooking.

title: Ensalada de Aguacate y Mango
description: A refreshing salad blending creamy avocado with sweet mango and crisp vegetables. Perfect for hot summer days.

steps.instruction: Dice avocado and mango into cubes. Slice cucumber and red onion thinly.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl. Drizzle with lime juice, olive oil, and season with salt and chili powder.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a crisp Sauvignon Blanc or a Mexican lager.
tips: Choose ripe but firm mangoes and avocados to avoid mushiness.
Let the salad sit for 10 minutes before serving to allow flavours to meld.

title: Lomo Saltado
description: A Peruvian stir-fry blending marinated beef, tomatoes, and onions with soy sauce and vinegar. Served with fries and rice.

steps.instruction: Cut beef into strips and marinate with salt, soy sauce, and garlic for 20 minutes. Cut potatoes into fries and fry until golden. Cook rice separately.
Sauté beef in oil over high heat until browned. Remove from pan.
In the same pan, stir-fry tomatoes and onions until softened. Add vinegar and return beef to the pan.
Serve beef mixture over rice, topped with fried potatoes and coriander.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a Peruvian white wine like Torontel or a light-bodied red like Malbec.

title: Feijoada
description: A rich, slow-cooked Brazilian stew of black beans and meats, served with rice, farofa, and collard greens. A Sunday classic.

steps.instruction: Soak black beans overnight. Sauté onion, garlic, smoked bacon, and sausage in oil until fragrant.
Add beans, beef short ribs, bay leaves, orange peel, and enough water to cover. Simmer for 3 hours until tender.
Remove meat, shred, and return to the pot. Season with salt.
Cook rice separately. Sauté cassava flour in oil until toasted for farofa. Blanch collard greens in boiling water.
Serve feijoada over rice, topped with farofa, collard greens, and a side of orange slices.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a bold Brazilian red wine like a Tannat or a Malbec.
tips: Use a pressure cooker to reduce cooking time to 1 hour.
For extra depth, add a dash of cachaca (Brazilian rum) to the pot.

title: Arepa de Queso
description: A beloved Venezuelan staple, arepas are grilled corn cakes stuffed with melted cheese. Perfect for breakfast or a quick meal.

steps.instruction: Mix cornmeal, salt, and water to form a soft dough. Let it rest for 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 balls, flatten into discs, and stuff with cheese.
Cook on a griddle over medium heat for 10 minutes per side, until golden and cheese melts.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs well with a light Venezuelan white wine like Vino de la Tierra de Uva Italia.
tips: Use pre-cooked cornmeal (masarepa) for authentic texture.
Serve with avocado slices for added creaminess.

title: Camarones al Ajillo con Yuca
description: A classic Peruvian dish featuring shrimp sautéed in garlic and chili, served with crispy yuca. A flavorful and hearty seafood dish.

steps.instruction: Peel and dice the yuca into 2 cm cubes. Boil in salted water for 15 minutes until tender. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a pan, add minced garlic and sliced chili. Sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add prawns and cook for 3 minutes per side until pink. Squeeze lemon juice and season with salt.
Fry the yuca cubes in a separate pan with a little oil until golden and crispy.

wine_pairing_notes: Pairs beautifully with a crisp Peruvian Pisco Sour or a dry white wine like Sauvignon Blanc.
tips: Use fresh, large prawns for the best flavor.
For extra crispiness, double-fry the yuca cubes.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Bruschetta al Pomodoro
description: Classic Tuscan starter featuring toasted bread topped with fresh tomatoes, basil, and garlic. A simple yet flavourful dish that highlights the quality of summer produce.

steps.instruction: Slice the ciabatta into 1 cm thick slices and toast in the oven at 180°C for 5 minutes until golden.
Dice the tomatoes and place in a bowl. Finely chop the basil and mince the garlic. Add both to the tomatoes along with olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix gently.
Spoon the tomato mixture onto the toasted bread slices and serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: A light Pinot Grigio complements the fresh flavours.
tips: Use the ripest tomatoes for the best flavour

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy risotto infused with wild mushrooms and Parmesan cheese. A comforting dish that brings the earthy flavours of autumn to the table.

steps.instruction: Clean the mushrooms and slice them. Finely chop the shallots and mince the garlic.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large pan. Sauté the shallots and garlic for 3 minutes until soft.
Add the mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the rice and stir for 2 minutes to toast lightly. Pour in the wine and let it evaporate completely.
Start adding the hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is absorbed before adding the next. This should take about 20 minutes.
When the rice is al dente, stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: A full-bodied white wine like Gavi di Gavi pairs beautifully.
tips: Stir constantly to release the starch and create a creamy texture

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Saltimbocca alla Romana
description: A classic Roman dish of veal cutlets topped with prosciutto and sage, cooked in white wine butter sauce. Quick to prepare but packed with flavour.

steps.instruction: Lay the veal cutlets between cling film and pound them thin with a meat mallet.
Place a slice of prosciutto and 2 sage leaves on each cutlet. Roll up and secure with a toothpick.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a frying pan. Brown the saltimbocca on all sides for 2-3 minutes total. Remove from pan and set aside.
Add the remaining butter to the pan. Pour in the wine and let it reduce by half. Return the saltimbocca to the pan and cook for another minute.
Serve immediately with a drizzle of the sauce.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied red like Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is ideal.
tips: Use very thin veal cutlets for best results

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Pasta alla Norma
description: A beloved Sicilian pasta dish featuring fried aubergines, ricotta salata, and a simple tomato sauce. The combination of textures and flavours is simply divine.

steps.instruction: Dice the aubergines and toss with 10g salt. Let sit for 10 minutes then pat dry with kitchen paper.
Fry the aubergines in 60ml olive oil in batches until golden brown. Drain on kitchen paper and sprinkle with salt.
Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water for 30 seconds, then peel and chop. Crush the garlic.
Heat remaining oil in a pan. Sauté the garlic for 1 minute then add the tomatoes and red pepper flakes. Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Cook the pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 100ml pasta water then drain.
Toss the pasta with the tomato sauce, adding pasta water as needed. Stir in the aubergines and basil. Serve with grated ricotta salata on top.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied Sicilian red like Nero d'Avola pairs perfectly.
tips: Salt the aubergines to remove bitterness and improve texture
Fry the aubergines in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Tiramisu Classico
description: The iconic Italian dessert of coffee-soaked ladyfingers layered with mascarpone cream and dusted with cocoa. A perfect end to any meal.

steps.instruction: Brew the coffee and let it cool. Whisk the egg yolks with 60g sugar until pale and thick. Add the mascarpone and mix until smooth.
Beat the egg whites with the remaining sugar until stiff peaks form. Fold gently into the mascarpone mixture.
Quickly dip each ladyfinger in the coffee (do not soak) and arrange a layer in a dish. Sprinkle with amaretto if using.
Spread half the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers. Repeat with another layer of dipped ladyfingers and cream.
Chill in the fridge for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight. Before serving, dust generously with cocoa powder and grate dark chocolate on top.

wine_pairing_notes: A sweet Vin Santo or espresso are traditional pairings.
tips: Use room temperature eggs for easier whisking
Chill for as long as possible for best texture

Tuck fresh basil leaves between the tomato and mozzarella slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Sprinkle with sea salt and black pepper.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Spaghetti alla Carbonara
description: Rome's iconic pasta dish made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper. Rich, creamy, and deeply flavourful without any cream.

steps.instruction: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a pan, fry diced guanciale on medium heat until crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
In a bowl, whisk eggs with grated Pecorino Romano and black pepper.
Add drained spaghetti to the pan with guanciale. Remove from heat, then quickly stir in the egg mixture, adding reserved pasta water as needed to create a creamy sauce.

description: A baked dish of fried aubergine layered with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan. Hearty, cheesy, and full of Mediterranean flavours.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A classic Roman dish with a simple yet flavorful combination of garlic, olive oil, and chili. Quick to prepare and perfect for a light meal.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Focaccia Genovese
description: A traditional Genoese focaccia with a soft, fluffy interior and a crispy, olive oil-kissed crust. Perfect as an appetizer or snack.

steps.instruction: In a small bowl, dissolve the yeast and sugar in warm water. Let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large bowl, mix the flour and salt. Make a well in the center and pour in the yeast mixture and olive oil. Stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 220°C (425°F). Grease a 20x30 cm baking tray with olive oil. Spread the dough out in the tray, pressing it to the edges. Dimple the dough with your fingertips.
Drizzle generously with olive oil, sprinkle with sea salt flakes and rosemary (if using). Let rise for another 20 minutes.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden brown. Serve warm or at room temperature.

tips: For extra flavor, add a few pitted olives or cherry tomatoes on top before baking.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A classic Neapolitan pasta dish with a simple yet flavorful garlic and oil sauce. It's quick to prepare and highlights the quality of the ingredients.

steps.instruction: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 100ml pasta water, then drain.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over low heat. Add whole garlic cloves and chili flakes, sauté until garlic is golden.
Remove pan from heat, discard garlic cloves. Toss pasta in the oil, adding reserved pasta water as needed to emulsify the sauce.
Stir in chopped parsley and salt to taste. Serve immediately with a drizzle of olive oil.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a crisp Italian white like Vermentino or a light red like Barbera.
tips: Use high-quality extra virgin olive oil for the best flavor.
For extra richness, add a pat of butter at the end.
Add a splash of pasta water to help the sauce cling to the pasta.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Risotto ai Funghi
description: A creamy, earthy risotto made with fresh wild mushrooms and Parmesan. Perfect for a cozy autumn or winter meal.

steps.instruction: Finely chop onion. Clean mushrooms and slice them. Keep stock warm in a pot.
Heat olive oil and 10g butter in a large pan. Sauté onion until soft. Add mushrooms and cook until they release their juices.
Add rice and toast for 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Pour in wine and let it evaporate.
Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently until absorbed before adding more. Continue for 16-18 minutes.
Remove from heat. Stir in remaining butter, Parmesan, salt, and pepper. Serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a full-bodied Italian red like Barolo or Valpolicella.
tips: Use a mix of wild mushrooms for deeper flavor (e.g., porcini, chanterelle).
Stir constantly in the last 10 minutes to achieve the perfect creamy texture.
For extra creaminess, stir in a knob of cold butter at the end.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Melanzane alla Parmigiana
description: A rich, layered casserole of fried aubergines, tomato sauce, and melted cheese. A hearty dish from Southern Italy.

steps.instruction: Slice aubergines into 1cm thick rounds. Salt both sides and let sit for 10 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry.
Heat olive oil in a pan. Fry aubergine slices in batches until golden. Drain on paper towels.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil, sauté garlic until fragrant. Add passata, season with salt and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 200°C. Layer aubergine slices in a baking dish, alternating with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan. Repeat layers.
Bake for 25 minutes or until bubbly and golden. Sprinkle with fresh basil and a drizzle of balsamic vinegar before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied Italian red like Chianti or Nero d'Avola.
tips: For extra flavor, grill aubergine slices instead of frying.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes after baking to set the layers.
Use fresh mozzarella for the best texture.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Pasta al Pesto di Rucola
description: A fresh and peppery pesto made with rocket (arugula) instead of basil, tossed with pasta. Quick and vibrant, perfect for summer.

steps.instruction: Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 100ml pasta water, then drain.
Toast pine nuts in a dry pan for 2 minutes until lightly golden.
Blend rocket, garlic, Parmesan, pine nuts, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a food processor. Stream in olive oil until smooth.
Toss pasta with pesto, adding reserved pasta water as needed to loosen the sauce.
Serve immediately with extra Parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a crisp Italian white like Vermentino or Pinot Grigio.
tips: For a vegan version, replace Parmesan with nutritional yeast.
Add a handful of cherry tomatoes halved for extra freshness.
Use fresh rocket for the brightest flavor.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Pollo alla Cacciatora
description: A rustic Tuscan stew of chicken braised with tomatoes, olives, and herbs. Hearty and comforting, ideal for cooler days.

steps.instruction: Chop onion, carrots, celery, and garlic finely. Heat olive oil in a large pan. Season chicken with salt and pepper, then brown on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic until softened. Add rosemary and black olives, cook 2 minutes.
Pour in white wine, let it reduce by half. Add chopped tomatoes, bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes until chicken is tender and sauce thickens.
Serve hot with crusty bread or polenta to soak up the sauce.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied red like Chianti or Sangiovese.
tips: Use bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs for the best flavor and texture.
For extra richness, add a few anchovies while cooking the vegetables.
Make ahead: The flavors improve the next day.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A simple yet flavorful dish that highlights the beauty of minimal ingredients. It's quick to prepare and bursting with garlic and olive oil essence.

steps.instruction: Cook the spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 100ml of pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a pan over low heat. Add minced garlic and chilli flakes, sautéing until garlic is golden (about 3 minutes).
Add the drained spaghetti to the pan, tossing well to coat. Add reserved pasta water as needed to emulsify the sauce.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped parsley, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a light Italian white wine like Vermentino or Pinot Grigio to complement the garlic and oil.
tips: Use high-quality olive oil for the best flavor.
Don’t overcook the garlic to avoid bitterness.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Eggplant Parmigiana
description: A layered, baked delight featuring crispy fried aubergine and rich tomato sauce. This dish is a hearty vegetarian classic.

steps.instruction: Slice the aubergine into 1cm thick rounds. Salt both sides and let sit for 20 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry with kitchen paper.
Set up three bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one with breadcrumbs. Bread each aubergine slice.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry aubergine slices until golden on both sides (about 3 minutes per side). Drain on paper towels.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil and sauté garlic until fragrant. Add tomato passata, oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes.
In a baking dish, layer aubergine slices with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan. Repeat layers, finishing with cheese on top.
Bake in a preheated oven at 190°C for 25 minutes or until bubbly and golden. Garnish with fresh basil before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied Italian red like Chianti or Sangiovese pairs beautifully with the rich, cheesy layers.
tips: For extra crispiness, double-fry the aubergine slices.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes after baking to help it set before serving.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy Arborio rice cooked with wild mushrooms and white wine for an umami-rich dish. This risotto is comforting and perfect for autumn evenings.

steps.instruction: Finely chop shallots and garlic. Clean mushrooms and slice into thin pieces.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté shallots and garlic until translucent (3 minutes).
Add mushrooms and thyme, cooking until mushrooms release their moisture and it evaporates (8 minutes).
Add Arborio rice to the pan, stirring to coat the grains with oil. Pour in the white wine and cook until fully absorbed.
Add heated stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently until the liquid is absorbed before adding more (about 20 minutes total).
Once the rice is creamy and al dente, stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

wine_pairing_notes: A full-bodied white wine like Gavi or a light red like Pinot Noir complements the earthy mushrooms.
tips: Use a good-quality Parmesan for the best flavor.
Keep the stock warm in a separate pan to maintain a consistent cooking temperature.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Caprese Salad
description: A fresh and vibrant salad with ripe tomatoes, creamy mozzarella, and fragrant basil. Simple yet iconic, perfect for summer.

steps.instruction: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into 1cm thick rounds. Arrange alternately on a plate.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the tomato and mozzarella slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

wine_pairing_notes: A crisp Italian white like Vermentino or a light rosé pairs perfectly with the fresh flavors.
tips: Use the ripest tomatoes you can find for the best flavor.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar sparingly to avoid overpowering the salad.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Tiramisu
description: A classic Italian dessert featuring layers of coffee-soaked sponge and mascarpone cream, dusted with cocoa. Decadent and delightful.

steps.instruction: Brew the coffee and let it cool. If using rum or Marsala, mix into the coffee.
Separate egg whites from yolks. Whisk yolks with 80g sugar until pale and thick (about 5 minutes).
In another bowl, beat egg whites with remaining sugar until stiff peaks form (about 5 minutes).
Gently fold mascarpone into the egg yolk mixture until smooth. Fold in egg whites until fully combined.
Dip each ladyfinger briefly into the coffee (just enough to moisten, not soak) and arrange in a single layer in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone mixture over the ladyfingers. Repeat with another layer of coffee-dipped ladyfingers and remaining cream.
Grate dark chocolate over the top and dust generously with cocoa powder. Chill for at least 4 hours (or overnight) before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a sweet dessert wine like Vin Santo or a coffee liqueur to enhance the coffee flavors.
tips: Use high-quality coffee for the best flavor.
Let the tiramisu chill for at least 4 hours to allow flavors to meld.
